figures.caption: Figure 3: Types of multilayer networks. The upper plot shows the classification tree of MLNs as presented in the this review. Below each category is an example of a multilayer public transport network. The leftmost plot represents MI represents an interdependent network where the bottom layer is the L-space metro and top layer L-space bus. The middle network MH , is an example of a heterogeneous network, where the bottom layer is again the L-space metro and the top layer is the C-space representation of the same network. The rightmost network, represents a multiplex network MM where the bottom layer is the L-space representation of the toy metro network and the top layer is the OD layer. (See also descriptions in the text).
Figure 1: An illustration of the standard PTN graph representations.
Figure 4: Supra-adjacency matrices for the interdependent toy network MI (left) and the multiplex toy network MM (right). The networks are visualised in Figure 3.
Table 2: Overview of the works included in the systematic review - multilayer-specific metrics. PT modes: M - metro, B - bus, R - rail, T - tram, F - ferry, A - air, W - water.
Figure 5: A two-level taxonomy of MLPTNs.

Figure 2: An illustration of a simple multilayer network consisting of two layers. Intra-layer edges within a single layer are shown in bold lines and inter-layer edges connecting nodes from separate layers are shown in dashed lines.
Table 4: Overview of the works included in the systematic review - optimisation. PT modes: M - metro, B - bus, T - tram.
Table 1: Overview of the works included in the systematic review - classical topological metrics extended to multilayer networks. PT modes: M - metro, B - bus, R - rail.
Table 5: Classification of related works according to the proposed taxonomy.

Table 3: Summary of surveyed studies on MLPTN resilience. F/R denote if failure and/or recovery phases are studied; V/E indicate whether nodes and/or edges are affected; “type of disruption/recovery” specifies the attack/recovery strategy, and “metrics” lists evaluated indicators.
